title: Submit first upstream correction(s) (Cellosaurus / Cell Model Passports)
context: Errors found in upstream records during cataloguing should flow back to the source resources, turning the catalog into a contributor rather than just a consumer. M0/M1 requires submitting the first upstream correction(s) with recorded evidence artifacts. Standing guardrails bind this task: open-access sources only (no dbGaP/EGA/biobank/controlled-access); COSMIC/OncoKB are link-only; donor fields are published-only with no re-identification or external linkage; every assertion carries field-level provenance; the deliverable is de-identified research metadata, never patient data and never a clinical/therapeutic recommendation.
objective: Submit the first upstream correction(s) to an upstream resource and record the submission as an outcome artifact.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one concrete correction is submitted to an upstream resource (Cellosaurus / Cell Model Passports / DepMap) via its self-serve channel.
Each submission records an outcomes/<event-id>.json (channel, permalink, timestamp, before/after) with field-level evidence.
Submissions derive only from documented, provenanced catalog findings (e.g. from misid-010/entries-009); none are speculative.
Submission counts toward M1; acceptance is tracked separately under corrections-017.
resources: ICLAC Register of Misidentified Cell Lines
output: outcomes/ submission artifact(s) plus an UPSTREAM-CORRECTIONS.md log of submitted corrections.
